title: The fountain where strangers leave coins
summary: Describe a small custom around a public fountain, where people leave or take coins for one another.
topic: A small custom around a public fountain
prompt: Write 8–10 sentences describing a small custom or habit that happens in a public place, such as people leaving coins, notes, or small gifts for strangers. Describe what people do, who started it, and how it makes you feel. Use present simple, past simple, and the connectors 'when' and 'so'.
hintStarters: In the square near my office, there is a small stone fountain where…
Nobody knows exactly when it started, but…
When someone leaves a coin on the edge, the next person who needs it…
The first time I took one, I felt…
sampleText: In the square near my office in Naples, there is a small stone fountain, and people leave coins on its wide edge. These coins are not wishes thrown into the water. They are for the next person who needs one. The custom is simple. If you have a little extra money, you leave a coin on the stone. If you are short of change for the bus or a coffee, you take one. Nobody watches, and nobody keeps count. I asked an old woman who sells newspapers nearby when it started. She said it began many years ago with one man who left a coin for a stranger, and slowly other people copied him. Now there are almost always a few coins waiting on the edge. The first time I took one, I felt a little ashamed, because I am not poor. But that day I had forgotten my wallet, and one coin was exactly enough for the bus home. The next week I came back and left three coins, so the fountain stayed full. What I like is that you never meet the person you help, and the person who helps you stays a stranger too. When trust works between people who will never know each other, the whole square feels softer.

sampleAnnotations.phrase: If you are short of change
Nobody watches, and nobody keeps count

sampleGrammarNotes.title: Zero conditional with 'if' for a general rule
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyVi: Câu 'If you have a little extra money, you leave a coin' dùng câu điều kiện loại 0: cả hai vế đều ở hiện tại đơn. Loại này diễn tả một quy tắc hoặc sự thật luôn đúng, không nói về một dịp cụ thể.
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyEn: The sentence 'If you have a little extra money, you leave a coin' uses the zero conditional: both halves are in the present simple. This type expresses a rule or a fact that is always true, not a single occasion.

sampleGrammarNotes.title: Past perfect for an earlier past action
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyVi: Câu 'that day I had forgotten my wallet, and one coin was exactly enough' dùng quá khứ hoàn thành ('had forgotten') cho hành động xảy ra trước một thời điểm quá khứ khác ('was'). Nó cho thấy thứ tự: quên ví trước, rồi đồng xu mới vừa đủ.
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyEn: The sentence 'that day I had forgotten my wallet, and one coin was exactly enough' uses the past perfect ('had forgotten') for an action before another past moment ('was'). It shows the order: forgetting the wallet came first, then the coin was just enough.

sampleTranslationVi: Ở quảng trường gần văn phòng tôi tại Naples, có một đài phun nước nhỏ bằng đá, và người ta để các đồng xu trên mép rộng của nó. Những đồng xu này không phải điều ước ném xuống nước. Chúng dành cho người tiếp theo cần đến. Tục lệ rất đơn giản. Nếu bạn có một chút tiền dư, bạn để một đồng xu lên đá. Nếu bạn thiếu tiền lẻ cho xe buýt hay một ly cà phê, bạn lấy một đồng. Không ai canh chừng, và không ai đếm. Tôi hỏi một bà cụ bán báo gần đó tục lệ bắt đầu khi nào. Bà nói nó bắt đầu nhiều năm trước với một người đàn ông để lại một đồng xu cho người lạ, và dần dần những người khác làm theo ông. Bây giờ gần như luôn có vài đồng xu chờ trên mép. Lần đầu tôi lấy một đồng, tôi thấy hơi xấu hổ, vì tôi không nghèo. Nhưng hôm đó tôi đã quên ví, và một đồng xu vừa đủ cho chuyến xe buýt về nhà. Tuần sau tôi quay lại và để ba đồng xu, để đài phun nước vẫn đầy. Điều tôi thích là bạn không bao giờ gặp người bạn giúp, và người giúp bạn cũng vẫn là người lạ. Khi sự tin tưởng vận hành giữa những người sẽ không bao giờ biết nhau, cả quảng trường trở nên dịu dàng hơn.

mcQuestions.prompt: Choose the best-written sentence about people leaving coins.
options: People leave coins on it's wide edge.
People leave coins on his wide edge.
People leave coins on its wide edge.
People leaves coins on its wide edge.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Its' is the possessive. 'It's' means 'it is'; 'his' is wrong for a fountain; 'leaves' disagrees with 'people'.

mcQuestions.prompt: Pick the correct zero-conditional sentence for the custom's rule.
options: If you will have extra money, you leave a coin.
If you have extra money, you will left a coin.
If you have extra money, you leaving a coin.
If you have extra money, you leave a coin.
mcQuestions.explanation: Zero conditional uses present simple in both halves. 'Will have' is wrong in the 'if' part; 'will left' mixes future and past; 'leaving' is not a full verb.
mcQuestions.hint: In a general rule, do we use 'will' after 'if'?

mcQuestions.prompt: Choose the most natural sentence about being short of change.
options: If you are short of change, you take one.
If you are short of change, you take it one.
If you are short for change, you take one.
If you are short of change, you takes one.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Short of change' and 'take one' are correct. 'Take it one' adds a wrong 'it'; 'short for' uses the wrong preposition; 'takes' disagrees with 'you'.
mcQuestions.hint: Which preposition follows 'short' when you lack something?

mcQuestions.prompt: Select the best sentence about how the custom began.
options: It began many years ago with one man who leaves a coin.
It began many years ago with one man who left a coin.
It begun many years ago with one man who left a coin.
It began many years ago with one man who had leaving a coin.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Began' and 'left' (past simple) match the time. 'Leaves' is present; 'begun' needs 'has'; 'had leaving' is wrong.
mcQuestions.hint: With 'many years ago', what tense fits?

mcQuestions.prompt: Choose the most natural sentence about others copying him.
options: Slow other people copied him.
Slowly other people copy him then.
Slowly other people copied him.
Slowly others people copied him.
mcQuestions.explanation: Adverb 'slowly' and past 'copied' are correct. 'Slow' is an adjective; 'copy … then' mixes present with a past marker; 'others people' is wrong ('other people').

mcQuestions.prompt: Pick the best sentence about how the writer felt.
options: The first time I took one, I felt a little ashaming.
The first time I take one, I felt a little ashamed.
The first time I took one, I feeled a little ashamed.
The first time I took one, I felt a little ashamed.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Felt a little ashamed' is correct. 'Ashaming' is not a word; 'take' should be past 'took'; 'feeled' is wrong ('felt').
mcQuestions.hint: What is the past tense of 'feel'?

mcQuestions.prompt: Choose the correct sentence using past perfect for an earlier action.
options: That day I had forgotten my wallet.
That day I had forget my wallet.
That day I have forgotten my wallet.
That day I had forgot my wallet.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Had forgotten' is the correct past perfect. 'Had forget' uses the base verb; 'have forgotten' is present perfect; 'had forgot' uses the wrong participle.
mcQuestions.hint: What is the past participle of 'forget'?

mcQuestions.prompt: Select the best sentence using 'so' to show the result.
options: I left three coins, because the fountain stayed full.
I left three coins, so the fountain stayed full.
I left three coins, so the fountain stay full.
I left three coins, so the fountain stayed fully.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'So' shows the result and 'stayed full' is correct. 'Because' shows reason; 'stay' drops the past tense; 'fully' is an adverb, but 'full' is the right adjective here.

mcQuestions.prompt: Choose the most natural sentence about never meeting the person.
options: You never meet the person who you help him.
You never meet the person whom you help him.
You never meet the person you help.
You never meet the person you helping.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'The person you help' is correct with the relative clause. Options 2 and 3 add an extra 'him'; 'you helping' is not a full verb.
mcQuestions.hint: Do you need an extra object 'him' after 'help' here?

mcQuestions.prompt: Pick the best closing sentence about the square feeling softer.
options: When trust works between people, the whole square feels more softer.
When trust work between people, the whole square feels softer.
When trust works among people, the whole square feel softer.
When trust works between people, the whole square feels softer.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Feels softer' is the correct comparative. 'More softer' doubles the comparative; 'work' drops the third-person 's'; 'feel' disagrees with 'square'.

criticalThinkingQuestion: Why might it feel easier to be kind to a stranger you will never meet than to someone you know well? What does an anonymous custom like this say about trust between people?
